title: lathyrus vestitus subsp. violaceus
intro: Lathyrus vestitus subsp. violaceus, commonly known as the purple vetchling, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. The plant contains a compound called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid), which has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to lower blood pressure and act as a diuretic. Research has also suggested that the plant may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, offering potential health benefits for individuals suffering from various diseases. In traditional herbal medicine, purple vetchling is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used in the form of infusions and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
